context.pr_title: Fix: Correct time-and-a-half pay calculation logic
context.pr_body:
### Describe your change:

- [x] Fix a bug or typo in an existing algorithm  

This pull request fixes the logic for calculating time-and-a-half pay in the `financial/time_and_half_pay.py` file.

Previously, the overtime calculation was incorrect. The fix correctly applies the time-and-a-half formula:
```python
overtime_pay = overtime_hours * pay_rate * 1.5
```


### Checklist:
* [x] I have read [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/TheAlgorithms/Python/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).
* [x] This pull request is all my own work -- I have not plagiarized.
* [x] I know that pull requests will not be merged if they fail the automated tests.
* [x] This PR only changes one algorithm file.  To ease review, please open separate PRs for separate algorithms.
* [x] All new Python files are placed inside an existing directory.
* [x] All filenames are in all lowercase characters with no spaces or dashes.
* [x] All functions and variable names follow Python naming conventions.
* [x] All function parameters and return values are annotated with Python [type hints](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html).
* [x] All functions have [doctests](https://docs.python.org/3/library/doctest.html) that pass the automated testing.
* [ ] All new algorithms include at least one URL that points to Wikipedia or another similar explanation.
* [ ] If this pull request resolves one or more open issues then the description above includes the issue number(s) with a [closing keyword](https://docs.github.com/en/issues/tracking-your-work-with-issues/linking-a-pull-request-to-an-issue): "Fixes #ISSUE-NUMBER".

agents.name: analysis_agent
agents.system_prompt:
You are an Analysis Agent specialized in Pull Request analysis. ## Project Context (MASCA Analysis) ## Project Analysis ### README

The Algorithms - Python


All algorithms implemented in Python - for education

Implementations are for learning purposes only. They may be less efficient than the implementations in the Python standard library. Use them at your discretion. ## Getting Started Read through our [Contribution Guidelines](CONTRIBUTING.md) before you contribute. ## Community Channels We are on [Discord](https://the-algorithms.com/discord) and [Gitter](https://gitter.im/TheAlgorithms/community)! Community channels are a great way for you to ask questions and get help. Please join us! ## List of Algorithms See our [directory](DIRECTORY.md) for easier navigation and a better overview of the project. ## Your Task When you receive a PR title and body, you must: 1. **Analyze the request**: Understand what the PR wants to achieve 2. **Explore the code**: Use tools to navigate base_project and find relevant files/functions 3. **Identify targets**: Determine exactly which files and functions need to be modified 4. **Optimize MASCA**: Produce a version of the MASCA analysis focused on the specific PR ## Available Tools - `read_base_project_file(file_path)`: Read a file from the project - `list_base_project_directory(directory, pattern, recursive)`: List files/folders ## Required Output You must produce a structured output with: - `pr_title`: PR title - `pr_body`: PR body - `masca_optimized`: MASCA optimized for this specific PR - `files_to_modify`: List of files to modify with motivation - `functions_to_modify`: List of functions to modify with motivation - `analysis_summary`: Summary of your analysis ## Guidelines - Explore the code before drawing conclusions - Search for files with names relevant to the PR - Identify specific functions, not just generic files - Be precise with paths (relative to base_project) - For function names use the format "ClassName.method_name" for methods

Analyze this Pull Request:

## Title
Fix: Correct time-and-a-half pay calculation logic

## Description
### Describe your change:

- [x] Fix a bug or typo in an existing algorithm  

This pull request fixes the logic for calculating time-and-a-half pay in the `financial/time_and_half_pay.py` file.

Previously, the overtime calculation was incorrect. The fix correctly applies the time-and-a-half formula:
```python
overtime_pay = overtime_hours * pay_rate * 1.5
```


### Checklist:
* [x] I have read [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/TheAlgorithms/Python/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).
* [x] This pull request is all my own work -- I have not plagiarized.
* [x] I know that pull requests will not be merged if they fail the automated tests.

* [x] All new Python files are placed inside an existing directory.
* [x] All filenames are in all lowercase characters with no spaces or dashes.
* [x] All functions and variable names follow Python naming conventions.
* [x] All function parameters and return values are annotated with Python [type hints](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html).
* [x] All functions have [doctests](https://docs.python.org/3/library/doctest.html) that pass the automated testing.
* [ ] All new algorithms include at least one URL that points to Wikipedia or another similar explanation.
* [ ] If this pull request resolves one or more open issues then the description above includes the issue number(s) with a [closing keyword](https://docs.github.com/en/issues/tracking-your-work-with-issues/linking-a-pull-request-to-an-issue): "Fixes #ISSUE-NUMBER".

arguments.reason: Inspect time-and-a-half pay implementation to locate bug and functions to change
arguments.expected_information: Function definitions, logic for overtime pay calculation, doctests, type hints, return types

tool_calls.raw_result:
"""
Calculate time and a half pay
"""


def pay(hours_worked: float, pay_rate: float, hours: float = 40) -> float:
    """
    hours_worked = The total hours worked
    pay_rate = Amount of money per hour
    hours = Number of hours that must be worked before you receive time and a half

    >>> pay(41, 1)
    41.5
    >>> pay(65, 19)
    1472.5
    >>> pay(10, 1)
    10.0
    """
    # Check that all input parameters are float or integer
    assert isinstance(hours_worked, (float, int)), (
        "Parameter 'hours_worked' must be of type 'int' or 'float'"
    )
    assert isinstance(pay_rate, (float, int)), (
        "Parameter 'pay_rate' must be of type 'int' or 'float'"
    )
    assert isinstance(hours, (float, int)), (
        "Parameter 'hours' must be of type 'int' or 'float'"
    )

    normal_pay = hours_worked * pay_rate
    over_time = max(0, hours_worked - hours)
    over_time_pay = over_time * pay_rate / 2
    return normal_pay + over_time_pay


if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Test
    import doctest

    doctest.testmod()
tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
This file implements a single function, pay, that calculates "time-and-a-half" wages (overtime pay) and includes doctests plus a __main__ block that runs those doctests.

### Architecture
- The module-level function pay(hours_worked: float, pay_rate: float, hours: float = 40) -> float is the only computation unit.
- Input validation is performed with assert statements to ensure inputs are numeric (int or float).
- The overtime calculation is done by computing a "normal" pay for all hours and then adding an additional half-pay for overtime hours.
- A small __main__ block calls doctest.testmod() to run the inline doctests.

### Functions & Classes
- pay — The sole function in the file.
  - Signature: pay(hours_worked: float, pay_rate: float, hours: float = 40) -> float
  - Purpose: Compute total pay including time-and-a-half overtime for hours worked beyond the threshold parameter hours (default 40).
  - Input validation: Uses assert isinstance(..., (float, int)) for each parameter; raises AssertionError with messages if types differ.
  - Implementation details:
    - normal_pay = hours_worked * pay_rate
      - This computes pay for all hours at the base rate.
    - over_time = max(0, hours_worked - hours)
      - This computes overtime hours (0 if none).
    - over_time_pay = over_time * pay_rate / 2
      - This computes the additional half-pay for each overtime hour.
    - return normal_pay + over_time_pay
      - Total pay = base pay for all hours + additional half-pay for overtime hours. This effectively yields 1.5 * pay_rate for overtime hours.
  - Doctests provided in the docstring:
    - >>> pay(41, 1)  -> 41.5
    - >>> pay(65, 19) -> 1472.5
    - >>> pay(10, 1)  -> 10.0
  - Importance: This is the central function to inspect and (most likely) modify when adapting overtime logic. All calculation logic and input checks live here.

- __main__ block — not named as a function but relevant:
  - Runs doctest.testmod() to validate examples in the docstring when the module is executed as a script.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- The agent's goal is to "Inspect time-and-a-half pay implementation to locate bug and functions to change." The relevant locations:
  - Function pay is the only place where overtime calculation occurs; any bug in time-and-a-half logic or any change required must be made here.
  - Key lines to inspect/change:
    - normal_pay = hours_worked * pay_rate
    - over_time = max(0, hours_worked - hours)
    - over_time_pay = over_time * pay_rate / 2
    - return normal_pay + over_time_pay
  - Potential issues / things to consider when modifying:
    - The current implementation computes pay for all hours at the base rate, then adds half-pay for overtime hours. Mathematically this yields the conventional time-and-a-half, but it is less explicit than splitting regular_hours and overtime_hours:
      - More explicit alternative: regular_hours = min(hours_worked, hours); overtime_hours = max(0, hours_worked - hours); regular_pay = regular_hours * pay_rate; overtime_pay = overtime_hours * pay_rate * 1.5; return regular_pay + overtime_pay
    - Input validation currently only enforces types (int or float). There is no check for negative values for hours_worked, pay_rate, or hours — these could be a source of bugs depending on desired behavior.
    - The parameter name hours is the threshold for overtime and could be renamed for clarity (e.g., overtime_threshold) if refactoring is allowed — but pay is the only function to update.
    - Doctests exist and should be updated if changing behavior or validation rules.
- Summary: Modify the pay function if any bug or behavior change is required; pay is the only function affecting overtime calculation and is the primary target for changes and additional validation/tests.

    normal_pay = hours_worked * pay_rate

    over_time_pay = over_time * pay_rate / 2
    return normal_pay + over_time_pay


if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Test
    import doctest

    doctest.testmod()

tool_calls.raw_result:
"""
Calculate time and a half pay
"""


def pay(hours_worked: float, pay_rate: float, hours: float = 40) -> float:
    """
    hours_worked = The total hours worked
    pay_rate = Amount of money per hour
    hours = Number of hours that must be worked before you receive time and a half

    >>> pay(41, 1)
    41.5
    >>> pay(65, 19)
    1472.5
    >>> pay(10, 1)
    10.0
    """
    # Check that all input parameters are float or integer
    assert isinstance(hours_worked, (float, int)), (
        "Parameter 'hours_worked' must be of type 'int' or 'float'"
    )
    assert isinstance(pay_rate, (float, int)), (
        "Parameter 'pay_rate' must be of type 'int' or 'float'"
    )
    assert isinstance(hours, (float, int)), (
        "Parameter 'hours' must be of type 'int' or 'float'"
    )

    normal_pay = hours_worked * pay_rate
    over_time = max(0, hours_worked - hours)
    over_time_pay = over_time * pay_rate / 2
    return normal_pay + over_time_pay


if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Test
    import doctest

    doctest.testmod()
tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
This module calculates "time and a half" pay for a worker given hours worked, an hourly pay rate, and a threshold hours value (default 40). It exposes a single function `pay` and includes doctests plus a small main guard that runs the doctests.

### Architecture
- Top-level module docstring: "Calculate time and a half pay".
- A single public function `pay(hours_worked: float, pay_rate: float, hours: float = 40) -> float` implements the computation.
- Input validation is performed with `assert` checks on types.
- The calculation is split into three local variables: `normal_pay`, `over_time`, and `over_time_pay`, and the function returns `normal_pay + over_time_pay`.
- A main guard (`if __name__ == "__main__":`) runs `doctest.testmod()` to execute the examples in the function docstring.

### Functions & Classes
`pay` — Calculate time-and-a-half pay; this is the only function in the file and the core of its behavior.
- Signature: `pay(hours_worked: float, pay_rate: float, hours: float = 40) -> float`
- Purpose: Compute the total pay where any hours beyond `hours` receive "time and a half" (1.5×) pay.
- Parameters:
  - `hours_worked`: total hours worked (accepted types: `int` or `float`).
  - `pay_rate`: hourly pay rate (accepted types: `int` or `float`).
  - `hours`: threshold hours before overtime applies (default `40`, accepted types: `int` or `float`).
- Input validation:
  - `assert isinstance(hours_worked, (float, int)), ("Parameter 'hours_worked' must be of type 'int' or 'float'")`
  - `assert isinstance(pay_rate, (float, int)), ("Parameter 'pay_rate' must be of type 'int' or 'float'")`
  - `assert isinstance(hours, (float, int)), ("Parameter 'hours' must be of type 'int' or 'float'")`
  - Note: These assertions only check types, not ranges or non-negativity.
- Calculation steps (local variables exactly as named in source):
  - `normal_pay = hours_worked * pay_rate`
    - This multiplies all hours by the base rate (counts overtime at the base rate as well).
  - `over_time = max(0, hours_worked - hours)`
    - Computes overtime hours (zero if none).
  - `over_time_pay = over_time * pay_rate / 2`
    - Computes the extra half-rate pay for overtime hours (0.5× pay_rate per overtime hour).
  - `return normal_pay + over_time_pay`
    - Total pay = all hours at base rate + extra half-rate for overtime, which yields 1.5× for overtime hours.
- Docstring examples / doctests inside `pay`:
  - `>>> pay(41, 1)` -> `41.5`
  - `>>> pay(65, 19)` -> `1472.5`
  - `>>> pay(10, 1)` -> `10.0`
- Main guard:
  - `if __name__ == "__main__": import doctest; doctest.testmod()`
  - Runs the doctests when the module is executed as a script.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
You asked to "Get exact source to propose correct patch". Relevant parts to inspect or modify are:
- The function `pay` (entire body): this is the precise implementation you will patch.
- The three key lines for the computation:
  - `normal_pay = hours_worked * pay_rate`
  - `over_time = max(0, hours_worked - hours)`
  - `over_time_pay = over_time * pay_rate / 2`
  - `return normal_pay + over_time_pay`
  These determine how overtime is calculated (current code adds half-rate pay for overtime on top of full-rate pay, giving effective 1.5×).
- The `assert` checks for parameter types if you plan to strengthen validation (e.g., check non-negative values or accept Decimal).
- The doctests in the `pay` docstring and the `doctest.testmod()` call if you plan to change expected outputs or add tests.

If your proposed patch will change overtime behavior, edge-case handling (negative inputs), or types/ranges, those are the exact lines and locations to reference.

summarization.user_prompt:
## File: financial/time_and_half_pay.py

## Agent's reason for reading this file:
Get exact source to propose correct patch

## What the agent expects to find:
Exact code, doctests, comments to reference lines to change

## Source Code:
"""
Calculate time and a half pay
"""


def pay(hours_worked: float, pay_rate: float, hours: float = 40) -> float:
    """
    hours_worked = The total hours worked
    pay_rate = Amount of money per hour
    hours = Number of hours that must be worked before you receive time and a half

    >>> pay(41, 1)
    41.5
    >>> pay(65, 19)
    1472.5
    >>> pay(10, 1)
    10.0
    """
    # Check that all input parameters are float or integer
    assert isinstance(hours_worked, (float, int)), (
        "Parameter 'hours_worked' must be of type 'int' or 'float'"
    )
    assert isinstance(pay_rate, (float, int)), (
        "Parameter 'pay_rate' must be of type 'int' or 'float'"
    )
    assert isinstance(hours, (float, int)), (
        "Parameter 'hours' must be of type 'int' or 'float'"
    )

    normal_pay = hours_worked * pay_rate
    over_time = max(0, hours_worked - hours)
    over_time_pay = over_time * pay_rate / 2
    return normal_pay + over_time_pay


if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Test
    import doctest

    doctest.testmod()

output.pr_title: Fix: Correct time-and-a-half pay calculation logic
output.pr_body:
### Describe your change:

- [x] Fix a bug or typo in an existing algorithm  

This pull request fixes the logic for calculating time-and-a-half pay in the `financial/time_and_half_pay.py` file.

Previously, the overtime calculation was incorrect. The fix correctly applies the time-and-a-half formula:
```python
overtime_pay = overtime_hours * pay_rate * 1.5
```


### Checklist:
* [x] I have read [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/TheAlgorithms/Python/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).
* [x] This pull request is all my own work -- I have not plagiarized.
* [x] I know that pull requests will not be merged if they fail the automated tests.
* [x] This PR only changes one algorithm file.  To ease review, please open separate PRs for separate algorithms.
* [x] All new Python files are placed inside an existing directory.
* [x] All filenames are in all lowercase characters with no spaces or dashes.
* [x] All functions and variable names follow Python naming conventions.
* [x] All function parameters and return values are annotated with Python [type hints](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html).
* [x] All functions have [doctests](https://docs.python.org/3/library/doctest.html) that pass the automated testing.
* [ ] All new algorithms include at least one URL that points to Wikipedia or another similar explanation.
* [ ] If this pull request resolves one or more open issues then the description above includes the issue number(s) with a [closing keyword](https://docs.github.com/en/issues/tracking-your-work-with-issues/linking-a-pull-request-to-an-issue): "Fixes #ISSUE-NUMBER".
output.masca_optimized:
Summary of PR intent
- The PR fixes the overtime calculation in financial/time_and_half_pay.py.
- The correct and explicit implementation should compute regular pay only for regular hours and compute overtime as overtime_hours * pay_rate * 1.5. Return regular_pay + overtime_pay.

Exploration performed
- Located file: financial/time_and_half_pay.py
- The module exposes a single function: pay(hours_worked: float, pay_rate: float, hours: float = 40) -> float
- Current implementation computes:
  normal_pay = hours_worked * pay_rate
  over_time = max(0, hours_worked - hours)
  over_time_pay = over_time * pay_rate / 2
  return normal_pay + over_time_pay
- While mathematically that yields effective 1.5× pay for overtime (base pay for all hours + half-pay extra for overtime), a change that replaces only the overtime line with overtime_pay = overtime_hours * pay_rate * 1.5 would overcount if normal_pay still uses hours_worked.

Targeted fix (what to change)
- Modify pay so it explicitly separates regular_hours and overtime_hours and computes:
  regular_hours = min(hours_worked, hours)
  overtime_hours = max(0.0, hours_worked - hours)
  regular_pay = regular_hours * pay_rate
  overtime_pay = overtime_hours * pay_rate * 1.5
  return regular_pay + overtime_pay
- Keep type assertions and doctests. The doctest expected outputs are unchanged by the explicit formulation.

Why this change
- Makes logic explicit and eliminates potential future errors from mixing full-pay-for-all-hours with a half-rate add-on change.
- Ensures any single-line replacement of overtime calculation cannot accidentally produce 2.5× pay for overtime hours.

Additional recommendations (optional)
- Consider adding non-negativity assertions for hours_worked, pay_rate, and hours (e.g., assert hours_worked >= 0) to prevent nonsensical inputs.
- Keep doctests as-is; run doctest.testmod() to verify behavior after change.

Files / functions to change
- Single file and function: financial/time_and_half_pay.py, function pay

Testing to run locally / CI
- Run python -m pytest (project tests) and run the module doctests (python -m doctest -v financial/time_and_half_pay.py or running the module directly). Verify the examples:
  >>> pay(41, 1) == 41.5
  >>> pay(65, 19) == 1472.5
  >>> pay(10, 1) == 10.0

Patch impact
- Minimal surface change: algorithm semantics remain the same for valid inputs, but the internal logic is clearer and safer against partial fixes that might erroneously overcount overtime.

files_to_modify.reason: This file contains the pay function that computes time-and-a-half pay. The current logic mixes base pay for all hours and an additional half-rate for overtime; to safely apply an explicit overtime formula (overtime_hours * pay_rate * 1.5) we must change the function implementation here to split regular and overtime hours and compute pay accordingly.

functions_to_modify.reason: pay currently computes normal_pay = hours_worked * pay_rate and then adds over_time_pay = over_time * pay_rate / 2. To correctly and explicitly apply the time-and-a-half formula without double-counting, modify pay to compute regular_hours and overtime_hours separately, then compute overtime_pay = overtime_hours * pay_rate * 1.5 and return regular_pay + overtime_pay.

output.analysis_summary: I inspected financial/time_and_half_pay.py and found the single function pay is the only place needing change. The current code computes base pay for all hours and then adds half-pay for overtime hours; although that produces the same numeric result as an explicit 1.5× overtime multiplier, replacing only the overtime line with overtime_pay = overtime_hours * pay_rate * 1.5 (without also adjusting normal_pay) would overcount overtime. The correct fix is to explicitly compute regular_hours = min(hours_worked, hours) and overtime_hours = max(0, hours_worked - hours), then compute regular_pay = regular_hours * pay_rate and overtime_pay = overtime_hours * pay_rate * 1.5 and return their sum. This single change lives in financial/time_and_half_pay.py, function pay. Update is minimal, keeps doctests unchanged, and avoids future regressions from partial edits.

agents.name: context_planner_agent
agents.system_prompt:
You are a Context Planner Agent specialized in step plan generation.

## Your Task
You receive from the Analysis Agent:
- PR title and body
- Optimized MASCA
- List of files to modify
- List of functions to modify

You must:
1. **Retrieve context**: Use tools to read the context files of the identified functions
2. **Analyze dependencies**: Understand how the functions interact
3. **Generate step plan**: Create a detailed step-by-step plan

## Available Tools
- `read_context_file(file_path)`: Read a pre-generated context file
- `list_context_files(directory, pattern)`: List available context files
- `read_call_graph(section)`: Read information from the call graph

## Required Output
You must produce a `step_plan` with:
- `summary`: 1-2 sentence summary of what the PR does
- `steps`: Ordered list of steps, each with:
  - `operation`: What to do (e.g., "Add validation method")
  - `file_to_modify`: Relative path of the file to modify (e.g., "src/module.py")
  - `function_to_modify`: Name of the function to modify (e.g., "ClassName.method_name")
  - `reason`: Technical motivation
  - `side_effects`: Potential impacts

## IMPORTANT RULE for function_to_modify
- If the file to modify is a Python file (.py), specify the function/method to modify
- If the file is NOT a Python file (e.g., .js, .html, .css, .json, .yaml, .md, etc.),
  the value of `function_to_modify` MUST be null

## Guidelines for Steps
- Each step must be atomic and implementable
- The order of steps must be logical (dependencies first)
- For Python files: always specify the function in the format "FunctionName" or "ClassName.method_name"
- For non-Python files: function_to_modify must be null
- The side_effects should mention tests, dependents, and API impacts
- You MUST generate a number of step EQUAL to the number of the python functions you are intendeed to modify nomore neither less

## Step Examples

### For Python files:
```json
{
  "operation": "Add time_spanned_alpha method",
  "file_to_modify": "manimlib/animation/animation.py",
  "function_to_modify": "Animation.time_spanned_alpha",
  "reason": "To compute animation progress based on actual elapsed time",
  "side_effects": "May require downstream animations to call this method"
}
```

### For non-Python files:
```json
{
  "operation": "Update API endpoint configuration",
  "file_to_modify": "config/routes.yaml",
  "function_to_modify": null,
  "reason": "Add new route for the authentication endpoint",
  "side_effects": "Requires server restart to apply changes"
}
```
agents.input_prompt:
Generate an implementation plan for this PR:

## PR Title
Fix: Correct time-and-a-half pay calculation logic

## PR Body
### Describe your change:

- [x] Fix a bug or typo in an existing algorithm  

This pull request fixes the logic for calculating time-and-a-half pay in the `financial/time_and_half_pay.py` file.

Previously, the overtime calculation was incorrect. The fix correctly applies the time-and-a-half formula:
```python
overtime_pay = overtime_hours * pay_rate * 1.5
```


### Checklist:
* [x] I have read [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/TheAlgorithms/Python/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).
* [x] This pull request is all my own work -- I have not plagiarized.
* [x] I know that pull requests will not be merged if they fail the automated tests.
* [x] This PR only changes one algorithm file.  To ease review, please open separate PRs for separate algorithms.
* [x] All new Python files are placed inside an existing directory.
* [x] All filenames are in all lowercase characters with no spaces or dashes.
* [x] All functions and variable names follow Python naming conventions.
* [x] All function parameters and return values are annotated with Python [type hints](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html).
* [x] All functions have [doctests](https://docs.python.org/3/library/doctest.html) that pass the automated testing.
* [ ] All new algorithms include at least one URL that points to Wikipedia or another similar explanation.
* [ ] If this pull request resolves one or more open issues then the description above includes the issue number(s) with a [closing keyword](https://docs.github.com/en/issues/tracking-your-work-with-issues/linking-a-pull-request-to-an-issue): "Fixes #ISSUE-NUMBER".

## MASCA Analysis (optimized)
Summary of PR intent
- The PR fixes the overtime calculation in financial/time_and_half_pay.py.
- The correct and explicit implementation should compute regular pay only for regular hours and compute overtime as overtime_hours * pay_rate * 1.5. Return regular_pay + overtime_pay.

Exploration performed
- Located file: financial/time_and_half_pay.py
- The module exposes a single function: pay(hours_worked: float, pay_rate: float, hours: float = 40) -> float
- Current implementation computes:
  normal_pay = hours_worked * pay_rate
  over_time = max(0, hours_worked - hours)
  over_time_pay = over_time * pay_rate / 2
  return normal_pay + over_time_pay
- While mathematically that yields effective 1.5× pay for overtime (base pay for all hours + half-pay extra for overtime), a change that replaces only the overtime line with overtime_pay = overtime_hours * pay_rate * 1.5 would overcount if normal_pay still uses hours_worked.

Targeted fix (what to change)
- Modify pay so it explicitly separates regular_hours and overtime_hours and computes:
  regular_hours = min(hours_worked, hours)
  overtime_hours = max(0.0, hours_worked - hours)
  regular_pay = regular_hours * pay_rate
  overtime_pay = overtime_hours * pay_rate * 1.5
  return regular_pay + overtime_pay
- Keep type assertions and doctests. The doctest expected outputs are unchanged by the explicit formulation.

Why this change
- Makes logic explicit and eliminates potential future errors from mixing full-pay-for-all-hours with a half-rate add-on change.
- Ensures any single-line replacement of overtime calculation cannot accidentally produce 2.5× pay for overtime hours.

Additional recommendations (optional)
- Consider adding non-negativity assertions for hours_worked, pay_rate, and hours (e.g., assert hours_worked >= 0) to prevent nonsensical inputs.
- Keep doctests as-is; run doctest.testmod() to verify behavior after change.

Files / functions to change
- Single file and function: financial/time_and_half_pay.py, function pay

Testing to run locally / CI
- Run python -m pytest (project tests) and run the module doctests (python -m doctest -v financial/time_and_half_pay.py or running the module directly). Verify the examples:
  >>> pay(41, 1) == 41.5
  >>> pay(65, 19) == 1472.5
  >>> pay(10, 1) == 10.0

Patch impact
- Minimal surface change: algorithm semantics remain the same for valid inputs, but the internal logic is clearer and safer against partial fixes that might erroneously overcount overtime.


## Files to Modify
- financial/time_and_half_pay.py: This file contains the pay function that computes time-and-a-half pay. The current logic mixes base pay for all hours and an additional half-rate for overtime; to safely apply an explicit overtime formula (overtime_hours * pay_rate * 1.5) we must change the function implementation here to split regular and overtime hours and compute pay accordingly.

## Functions to Modify
- pay (financial/time_and_half_pay.py): pay currently computes normal_pay = hours_worked * pay_rate and then adds over_time_pay = over_time * pay_rate / 2. To correctly and explicitly apply the time-and-a-half formula without double-counting, modify pay to compute regular_hours and overtime_hours separately, then compute overtime_pay = overtime_hours * pay_rate * 1.5 and return regular_pay + overtime_pay.

## Analysis Summary
I inspected financial/time_and_half_pay.py and found the single function pay is the only place needing change. The current code computes base pay for all hours and then adds half-pay for overtime hours; although that produces the same numeric result as an explicit 1.5× overtime multiplier, replacing only the overtime line with overtime_pay = overtime_hours * pay_rate * 1.5 (without also adjusting normal_pay) would overcount overtime. The correct fix is to explicitly compute regular_hours = min(hours_worked, hours) and overtime_hours = max(0, hours_worked - hours), then compute regular_pay = regular_hours * pay_rate and overtime_pay = overtime_hours * pay_rate * 1.5 and return their sum. This single change lives in financial/time_and_half_pay.py, function pay. Update is minimal, keeps doctests unchanged, and avoids future regressions from partial edits.

Use the context files to understand dependencies and generate a detailed step-by-step plan.

steps.operation: Refactor pay to explicitly compute regular_hours and overtime_hours and apply time-and-a-half formula for overtime
steps.reason: Current implementation calculates normal_pay = hours_worked * pay_rate and then adds half-rate pay for overtime (over_time_pay = overtime * pay_rate / 2). While numerically equivalent today, that pattern is fragile: replacing only the overtime term with overtime_hours * pay_rate * 1.5 would double-count. Explicitly splitting regular_hours and overtime_hours and computing regular_pay = regular_hours * pay_rate and overtime_pay = overtime_hours * pay_rate * 1.5 makes the logic clear and prevents future accidental overcounting.
steps.side_effects: No public API signature changes; doctests and expected outputs should remain the same for valid inputs. Tests should be run (pytest and module doctests) to verify behavior: e.g., pay(41, 1) == 41.5, pay(65, 19) == 1472.5, pay(10, 1) == 10.0. Potential impacts: callers that rely on current (possibly undefined) behavior for negative or non-finite inputs may observe different results — consider adding input validation (assert hours_worked >= 0, pay_rate >= 0) as a follow-up. Downstream code that inspects intermediate variables (if any) is unlikely to be affected because only internal calculation changes; no change to return type or function signature.

step_plan.summary: Make the time-and-a-half calculation explicit by splitting regular and overtime hours in financial/time_and_half_pay.py: compute regular_pay for up-to-threshold hours and overtime_pay as overtime_hours * pay_rate * 1.5, then return their sum.
